name: Mathily
parent_org: Mathematical Sciences Research Institute (MSRI) / Brandeis University

outcomes.college_impact: Positive signal, particularly for students applying to top liberal arts colleges and universities with strong math departments.
outcomes.typical_results: Most students pursue STEM majors; strong representation at schools like MIT, Harvard, and smaller liberal arts colleges.

what_they_look_for: Genuine interest in exploring advanced mathematical ideas

blurbs.short: A 5-week intensive summer mathematics program at Harvard focusing on combinatorics and discrete math.
blurbs.long: Mathily (Mathematics Summer Institute) is a focused, small-group summer program with a strong emphasis on combinatorics and discrete mathematics. The program is run by the Mathematical Sciences Research Institute (MSRI) and has been held at various locations including Harvard. With a cohort of only 35 students, Mathily is intentionally intimate — students work closely with faculty and each other. The program is known for its emphasis on original mathematical research and student presentations. It's a good fit for students who enjoy competition math (AMC/AIME) but want to see what real mathematical research feels like.

curriculum: Combinatorics, graph theory, discrete math, student research projects, math talks.
